name: Focaccia Florentine
recipeIngredient: 2 packages (10 oz each) frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a skillet. Add spinach, bacon, and 1/4 teaspoon salt; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat.
2. In a large bowl, combine 1 cup flour, yeast, and remaining salt. In a saucepan, heat water and the remaining oil until very warm (125-130°F).
3. Gradually beat in the water mixture into the flour mixture. Using a wooden spoon, gradually mix in 1 1/2 cups flour, about half a cup at a time, to form a soft dough.
4. Knead the dough on a floured surface until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes, adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking.
5. Shape the dough into a ball. Cover and let rest for 10 minutes.
6. On a floured surface, roll the dough into a 15x11-inch rectangle. Place it in a greased 15x11x1-inch jelly-roll pan, gently pressing it up into the corners.
7. Using your finger, make indentations all over the surface of the dough, almost to the bottom of the pan. Scatter the spinach and sprinkle with cheese.
8. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place until nearly doubled in size, about 30 minutes.
9. Preheat oven to 400°F. Bake for 20-25 minutes, until lightly browned. Cut into 12 pieces. Serve warm.
